design_decisions.choice: D1 — Transition mechanism is NORMALISE-BEFORE-VALIDATE, not tolerant-validate: one read-path normalise() shim maps retired quadrant chore->quick / epic->beast, strips schema_version, and reshapes legacy board tracks[] AND top-level release fields into canonical v0.10.0 form BEFORE validation. Validate and additionalProperties:false stay strictly strict; the vendored schemas are byte-identical to the tag; S12 deletes the shim wholesale.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — ratified in spec rationale + captain-proceed.md pin 1; keeps drift detectable during the S11->S12 window.
design_decisions.rationale: A weakened Validate would silently accept genuine drift; normalise on read preserves strict validation while letting un-migrated records still parse until S12 migrates the data.

design_decisions.choice: Pin-1 — EXPAND board pure-plan to the RELEASE level: the board writer stops persisting top-level schema_version, release_worktree_path, release_worktree_branch; the reader DERIVES the release worktree branch (release-wt/<release>) and path (sibling-of-repo); the normalise shim strips the three from legacy board.json on read. Required so a byte-identical v0.10.0 board-v1 (additionalProperties:false) accepts freshly-written boards.
options: Extend sworn#80 derivation to the release level (chosen)
Retain release_worktree_* in a sworn-local, non-byte-identical board-v1
Defer with a Coach-acknowledged Rule-2 window tolerance
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — the critical escalate pin, ratified live in captain-proceed.md; spec amended on release-wt @e8ecbd5 (AC-06/AC-07 + in_scope).
design_decisions.rationale: Byte-identical v0.10.0 board-v1 drops the top-level fields; the writer still emitted them and WriteBoard validates every write, so a byte-identical vendor would make every board write schema-invalid. The release worktree branch/path are as derivable as the track ones.

design_decisions.choice: D2 — Track AND release path derivation REUSE worker.go's sibling-of-release-worktree logic (defaultTrackWorktreePath, eval finding 3), moved to a shared internal/board helper; NOT the naive $HOME/projects/<repo>-worktrees formula. Track state is derived via git merge-base --is-ancestor (git.Repo.IsAncestor), the same primitive /merge-track uses.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — confirmed in captain-proceed.md pin 6; spec R-05/AC-07.
design_decisions.rationale: The naive $HOME formula caused eval finding 3's cross-repo collision (a fired-repo run materialised on sworn's worktree tree). The repo-local sibling path is the correct derivation and must be the single source of truth.

design_decisions.choice: D3 — sworn#80: board-v1 tracks[] loses worktree_path/worktree_branch/state as persisted fields (upstream-first, v0.10.0); internal/board computes them. Scope bounded to exactly these three track fields (plus the three release-level fields of Pin-1); baton#55 owns the remaining board-v1 canonical-conformance gaps.
options: Remove the three fields as persisted data, derive them (chosen)
Reshape to upstream's nested worktree{path,branch} object
design_decisions.rationale: Removing the fields moots the flattened-vs-nested shape question baton#54 flagged and eliminates the release-wt write race two concurrent /implement-slice sessions hit.

design_decisions.choice: D4 — The two new v0.10.0 schemas (contracts-v1, assembly-proof-v1) are VENDORED-ADVISORY: stored in internal/baton/schemas (root B only, Pin 3), byte-matched to the published $id, doctor-declarable, NOT graded. No `sworn lint contracts` / `sworn assemble` grader is wired here; do not fork the shape under the same $id.
options: Do not vendor until the graders land
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — AC-10/AC-11 + captain-proceed.md pin 3; the graders are the follow-on contract-edge-gates release.
design_decisions.rationale: Revendoring to v0.9.0 would ship an already-stale pin and reopen the schema-skew scar (baton#54/#55/#58); vendoring the two new schemas advisory keeps the binary's set aligned to the tag without pulling the grader scope forward.

design_decisions.choice: D5 — AC-08 write-isolation is SATISFIED-BY-ENGINE: sworn vendors no commands/ directory (the implement-slice.md/merge-track.md prose lives in the private ~/.claude harness + upstream baton, per the ADR-0010 boundary). sworn's obligation is Go behaviour only — no engine code path writes a track's worktree identity/state to release-wt's board.json (owned by AC-06/AC-07). The command-spec prose edit is tracked as baton#61.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — boundary already set by ADR-0010; captain-proceed.md pin 8.
design_decisions.rationale: sworn vendors the contract, not the workflow prose; the no-commands evidence test plus the AC-06/AC-07 write-isolation tests are the correct proof.

design_decisions.choice: D6 — Do NOT author records in quick/beast on disk yet: the binary accepts chore/grind/puzzle/epic until this slice lands v0.10.0's quick/beast via the normalise shim (sworn#90). S11's own status.json/spec.json quadrant fields stay legacy-valid (epic) until the enum flip + normalise land atomically in-track; S12 migrates the on-disk data. confirmed_by_implementer flips true now, recorded against the legacy epic value.
design_decisions.human_decision: Coach (Brad) 2026-07-11 — sequencing default; captain-proceed.md pin (P4 acknowledged).
design_decisions.rationale: Writing quick/beast before the enum flip lands would produce records the live binary rejects; keeping S11's records legacy-valid until the in-track flip avoids a self-inflicted invalid-record window.

effort_complexity.rationale: Vendor bump to baton v0.10.0 + chore->quick/epic->beast enum with a strict Validate fed by a read-path normalise shim + acknowledged_by round-trip + sworn#80 (derive track AND release worktree/state from refs, board-v1 pure plan) + two advisory schemas + Defect-2 regression lock; complexity high per the worker.go worktree-derivation nuance (spec R-05). Quadrant recorded as legacy 'epic' per D6 (maps to 'beast' post-flip via normalise; S12 migrates the on-disk value).

reachability_artifacts: cli-run: sworn board --release 2026-06-28-driver-contract -> EXIT 0 (derives track states from git ancestry)
cli-run: sworn designfit 2026-06-28-driver-contract -> DESIGNFIT PASS, EXIT 0 over 15 slices via the normalise shim (sworn#90)
test: internal/baton TestNormaliseRealRecordsValidateStrict (real board.json+spec.json fail strict as-authored, pass after Normalise) + internal/baton/schemas TestAdvisorySchemasByteMatchPublished
